description: Add thorough E2E tests for the create-opentabs-plugin scaffolding CLI, the opentabs plugin create/list/add/remove commands, and the opentabs build pipeline. Each test must use isolated temp directories and config dirs with proper cleanup so no garbage data is left behind.

userStories.title: E2E test: create-opentabs-plugin scaffolds a valid plugin project
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an E2E test that runs the actual `create-opentabs-plugin` CLI binary and verifies the full scaffolding flow produces a valid, buildable plugin project.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates an isolated temp directory and sets OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR to prevent touching real config
Test spawns `bun platform/create-plugin/dist/index.js <name> --domain <domain>` as a child process
Test verifies all 7 expected files are created (package.json, tsconfig.json, eslint.config.ts, .prettierrc, .gitignore, src/index.ts, src/tools/example.ts)
Test verifies package.json has correct name format (opentabs-plugin-<name>), correct dependencies (@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk, @opentabs-dev/cli)
Test verifies src/index.ts contains the correct class name (PascalCase), url pattern, and default export
Test verifies src/tools/example.ts contains a defineTool call with valid Zod schemas
Test verifies the plugin was auto-registered in the isolated config.json
Test verifies exit code is 0
Test verifies --display and --description options are reflected in generated code
Test verifies domain '.example.com' produces wildcard URL pattern '*://*.example.com/*'
Test verifies domain 'example.com' produces exact URL pattern '*://example.com/*'
All temp directories and config dirs are cleaned up in afterEach/afterAll (rmSync with recursive+force)
Tests use bun:test (not Playwright) since no browser is needed
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: The binary is at platform/create-plugin/dist/index.js. Use child_process spawn with bun to execute it. Follow the cleanup pattern from platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts (mkdtempSync + beforeEach/afterEach + rmSync). Set OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR env var to the temp dir to isolate config writes. The test file should go in platform/create-plugin/src/index.test.ts. The existing scaffold.test.ts in platform/cli/ tests the scaffoldPlugin() function directly — this test should test the actual CLI binary end-to-end. Make sure tsconfig.test.json exists for create-plugin to include test files. Use Bun.spawnSync or child_process.execSync to run the CLI synchronously for simpler assertions.

userStories.title: E2E test: create-opentabs-plugin error handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests that verify the create-opentabs-plugin CLI handles error cases correctly (invalid name, existing directory, missing --domain).
acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies invalid plugin name (uppercase, reserved words) exits with code 1 and prints error to stderr
Test verifies running in a directory where the target name already exists exits with code 1 and prints 'already exists' error
Test verifies missing --domain flag exits with code 1 and prints usage error
All temp directories are cleaned up in afterEach
Tests use bun:test
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: Same test file as US-001 (platform/create-plugin/src/index.test.ts). Spawn the CLI binary and check exit codes and stderr output. Create a pre-existing directory before running to test the 'already exists' case. Use the same temp dir isolation pattern.

userStories.title: E2E test: scaffolded plugin can be installed and built
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an E2E test that verifies a scaffolded plugin can actually be installed (bun install) and built (bun run build) successfully, producing a valid opentabs-plugin.json manifest and adapter.iife.js.
acceptanceCriteria: Test scaffolds a plugin using the CLI binary (reuse pattern from US-001)
Test runs `bun install` in the scaffolded plugin directory
Test runs `bun run build` in the scaffolded plugin directory (which runs tsc && opentabs build)
Test verifies opentabs-plugin.json exists and is valid JSON with correct name, version, tools array
Test verifies dist/adapter.iife.js exists and is non-empty
Test verifies opentabs-plugin.json has adapterHash field
Test verifies the manifest's tools array has at least one tool with name, description, input_schema, output_schema
All temp directories are cleaned up (including node_modules in the scaffolded plugin)
Test has a longer timeout (60s) since bun install + tsc + opentabs build takes time
Tests use bun:test
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: This is the most important test — it validates the full create → install → build pipeline. Use a temp directory, scaffold the plugin, then run bun install and bun run build via Bun.spawnSync. Parse the opentabs-plugin.json to verify its structure. This test will be slower (~30-60s) so give it an appropriate timeout. The opentabs build command is at platform/cli/dist/cli.js. The scaffolded plugin's package.json references @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk and @opentabs-dev/cli as npm dependencies, so bun install must succeed (they are published packages). Add the test to the same file (platform/create-plugin/src/index.test.ts) or a separate file if that makes more sense.

userStories.title: E2E test: opentabs plugin list/add/remove commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests for the opentabs plugin list, opentabs plugin add, and opentabs plugin remove CLI commands to verify they correctly manage the config.json.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates an isolated OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR temp directory
Test runs `opentabs plugin add <path>` and verifies the path appears in config.json
Test runs `opentabs plugin add <same-path>` and verifies duplicate is detected (prints 'already configured')
Test runs `opentabs plugin list --json` and verifies JSON output contains the added plugin
Test runs `opentabs plugin remove <path>` and verifies the path is removed from config.json
Test verifies `opentabs plugin remove <nonexistent>` exits with code 1
All temp directories and config dirs are cleaned up in afterEach
Tests use bun:test
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: The CLI binary is at platform/cli/dist/cli.js. Run commands via Bun.spawnSync with OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR set. The test file should go in platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.test.ts. For the 'list --json' test, parse stdout as JSON and verify the structure. For add/remove, read the config.json file directly to verify mutations. Create a minimal plugin directory (just a folder with opentabs-plugin.json) so 'list' shows it as ready.

userStories.title: E2E test: opentabs build validates and builds a plugin
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests for the `opentabs build` command that verify it validates the plugin, bundles the IIFE, generates the manifest, and handles validation errors correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Test uses the existing e2e-test plugin (plugins/e2e-test) as a known-good baseline
Test copies the e2e-test plugin to a temp directory to avoid modifying the original
Test runs `opentabs build` in the copied plugin directory and verifies exit code 0
Test verifies opentabs-plugin.json is generated with correct structure (name, version, tools, adapterHash, url_patterns)
Test verifies dist/adapter.iife.js is generated and contains IIFE wrapper (starts with '(' or contains '(function')
Test verifies adapter.iife.js contains __adapterHash assignment
Test verifies adapter.iife.js contains Object.freeze call
Test modifies a copied plugin to have an invalid name and verifies opentabs build exits with code 1 and reports validation error
Test modifies a copied plugin to have no tools and verifies opentabs build exits with code 1
All temp directories are cleaned up in afterEach
Tests use bun:test
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: The opentabs build command runs from process.cwd(), so spawn it with cwd set to the copied plugin directory. The CLI binary is at platform/cli/dist/cli.js (run as `bun platform/cli/dist/cli.js build`). To create an invalid plugin for error tests: copy the e2e-test plugin, modify dist/index.js to change the name to 'INVALID' or remove tools. The e2e-test plugin source is at plugins/e2e-test/. Use cpSync to copy it to a temp dir. The test file should go in platform/cli/src/commands/build-e2e.test.ts (separate from the existing build.test.ts which tests validatePlugin unit). Remember to run tsc in the copied plugin first if modifying source, OR modify the compiled dist/index.js directly for validation error tests.
